portfolio: Threat model, input sanitisation, action execution safety, config trust
notes: ADR-003 Threat Model draft filed at docs/adr/003-threat-model.md; awaits peer review by council-architect, council-quartermaster, council-surgeon, council-intel. All three Wave 2 commander directives folded in: (1) first-run trust prompt codified as normative with SHA-256 hash over the canonicalised action set only (description edits do NOT re-prompt; any change to what SCOUT would run DOES re-prompt); non-TTY launches refuse rather than prompt silently. (2) Two shell-escape seams named and scoped — `print` step stdout (POSIX-single-quoted, NUL/newline refused) and explicit `["sh", "-c", ...]` opt-in (ceremonial `unsafe_shell_template = true` required when combined with `{placeholder}`); everywhere else is argv-only execvp. (3) Unknown placeholder = parse error; env set by a failed step = undefined (not empty) — later references abort the action with `action.failed{kind=undefined_env}`. Also specified: trust/sanitise/refuse table; closed placeholder set deferred to ADR-004; DB file permissions 0600 with parent 0700 on Unix, owner-check on every open, warn-but-continue on mode drift, no auto-chmod; `O_NOFOLLOW` on both config and DB; 256 KiB config size cap; PATH sanitised (drop `.` and empty entries) before execvp; control-byte stripping at render boundary; rusqlite bundled required (closes libsqlite3 supply surface); install must not drop a default config (would bypass the trust prompt). Out-of-scope list explicit: local attacker at user UID, side channels, supply chain (ADR-002 owns), network (offline-only), multi-user shared installs, downstream tool bugs, ratatui/crossterm render vulns, TOCTOU between index and action, wedged FUSE/NFS, data loss (Surgeon owns), cross-machine index sync, user-weakened umask on non-DB files, per-execution audit logging. Standing by for Wave 3 peer review of ADR-001 (already drafted), ADR-002, and ADR-004.
